---
title: "Neuroscience"
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-header">
  <h1>Neuroscience</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">Internal states, circuits, and the behaviour they produce.</p>
</div>

<div class="research-grid">

  <div class="research-card">
    <span class="output-tag">in revision</span>
    <h3>Ethomics &mdash; What Is a Hungry Fly?</h3>
    <p>
      High-dimensional behavioural state benchmarking in <em>Drosophila</em>.
      The Espresso platform captures 17 behavioural metrics simultaneously across
      hundreds of flies. Used here to ask: does a serotonergic circuit manipulation
      produce genuine hunger, or just a superficially similar signature?
    </p>
    <a href="02_ethomics.html">&rarr; Read more</a>
    <span style="color: var(--bs-secondary); font-size: 0.85em;">Interactive demo coming soon.</span>
  </div>

  <div class="research-card">
    <span class="output-tag">Current Biology 2017</span>
    <h3>Intertemporal Choice</h3>
    <p>
      Dorsal raphe serotonergic neurons bidirectionally control delay discounting
      in <em>Drosophila</em>. Activation increases patience; silencing increases
      impulsivity. Effect is trade-off specific &mdash; not a motor or motivational confound.
    </p>
    <a href="03_itc.html">&rarr; Read more</a>
  </div>

  <div class="research-card">
    <span class="output-tag">PNAS 2020</span>
    <h3>Noradrenergic Attention</h3>
    <p>
      Two distinct noradrenergic pathways implement different attentional control
      mechanisms in rodents. Selective lesions reveal a functional dissociation
      not apparent from anatomy alone.
    </p>
    <a href="cv.html">&rarr; Publication</a>
  </div>

  <div class="research-card stub">
    <span class="output-tag">In development</span>
    <h3>Epitranscriptomics</h3>
    <p>
      Direct RNA nanopore sequencing pipeline for m6A and pseudouridine mapping
      in <em>Drosophila</em> serotonergic neurons. Does hunger change the RNA
      modification landscape?
    </p>
  </div>

  <div class="research-card">
    <span class="output-tag">Nature Neuroscience 2016</span>
    <h3>Basolateral Amygdala Valence Circuits</h3>
    <p>
      Antagonistic negative and positive neurons of the basolateral amygdala
      encode opposing valence signals. Selective activation and silencing
      reveal a functional opponent architecture for fear and reward.
    </p>
    <a href="cv.html">&rarr; Publication</a>
  </div>

  <div class="research-card">
    <span class="output-tag">bioRxiv 2026</span>
    <h3>Octopamine, Dopamine, and the Hungry Fly Circuit</h3>
    <p>
      A mushroom-body output neuron (MBON11) integrates octopaminergic and dopaminergic
      signals to control hunger-motivated feeding in <em>Drosophila</em>.
      Octopaminergic VPM3/4 neurons are instructive but not required for hunger-driven
      feeding; dopaminergic PPL101 neurons are required but not instructive.
      MBON11 sits at the convergence point.
    </p>
    <a href="https://doi.org/10.64898/2026.03.13.711740">&rarr; bioRxiv</a>
  </div>

</div>
```
